---
title: "Figure 5 (macro_IR) — incremental covariance loss per interval step, normalised by the increment"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
Discrete derivative of the per-recording parameter variance with respect to the measurement interval:
for each consecutive pair (Delta -> Delta_next, increment = Delta_next/Delta = 2 or 2.5),
    y = [ var(Delta_next) / var(Delta) ] / (Delta_next / Delta)
i.e. the variance-loss factor per unit interval increment. Reference y = 1: variance grows in proportion
to the interval. y > 1: you lose MORE than proportional -> refining the interval here pays. y < 1: less
than proportional -> near saturation / oversampling. macro_IR, noise 0.1, 433ed13,
Likelihood_Fisher_Covariance diagonal, all params, all N_ch. Plotted at the geometric midpoint of each pair.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r data}
COV      <- "Probit_statistics_Gaussian_Distortion_Corrected_Covariance"   # gaussian run (1c2ae6f); Likelihood_ is empty there
PIDX     <- c(on = 0, off = 1, unitary_current = 2, Num_ch_mean = 5)
PARAM_LAB<- c(on = "log[10]~k[on]", off = "log[10]~k[off]",
              unitary_current = "log[10]~i", Num_ch_mean = "log[10]~N[ch]")

d <- map_dfr(Sys.glob("../data/1c2ae6f/figure_3_G_nch_*_nsim_10000_macro_IR_noise_0.1_battery_pool_G.csv"),
             ~ read.csv(.x, skip = 1, stringsAsFactors = FALSE))

v <- d %>%
  filter(component_path == COV, statistic == "value", probit == "mean",
         param_index == param_col, param_index %in% PIDX) %>%
  mutate(param_name = names(PIDX)[match(param_index, PIDX)]) %>%
  group_by(Num_ch, interval_in_tau, param_name) %>%
  summarise(var = mean(value), .groups = "drop")

dd <- v %>% group_by(Num_ch, param_name) %>% arrange(interval_in_tau) %>%
  mutate(iv_next = lead(interval_in_tau), var_next = lead(var),
         increment = iv_next / interval_in_tau,
         loss = var_next / var,
         y = log(loss) / log(increment),     # elasticity: d log(var)/d log(interval) = local exponent
         iv_mid = sqrt(interval_in_tau * iv_next)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(y)) %>% ungroup() %>%
  mutate(nch_f = factor(Num_ch),
         param = factor(param_name, levels = names(PIDX), labels = unname(PARAM_LAB)))
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 7.5, fig.height = 5.6}
g <- ggplot(dd, aes(iv_mid, y, colour = nch_f, group = nch_f)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey55", linewidth = .3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, colour = "grey45", linewidth = .3, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_line(linewidth = .5) + geom_point(size = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ param, nrow = 2, labeller = label_parsed) +
  scale_x_log10(breaks = c(.02, .1, .5)) +
  scale_colour_viridis_d(name = expression(N[ch]), end = .92, direction = -1) +
  labs(x = expression(Delta / tau[min]~"(geometric midpoint of the step)"),
       y = "loss elasticity   d log(variance) / d log(interval)") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 9, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), legend.position = "right",
        axis.text = element_text(size = 7.5), axis.title = element_text(size = 8.5),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 8.5))
g
ggsave("Figure_5_IR_interval_loss.pdf", g, width = 7.5, height = 5.6)
```

```{r table}
# where does refining pay (y>1) vs not, median over N_ch, per param and step
dd %>% group_by(param_name, iv_mid = round(iv_mid, 3)) %>%
  summarise(y = round(median(y), 2), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = param_name, values_from = y) %>% as.data.frame()
```
